# ViewPhoto.TV — Full Reference

## What is ViewPhoto.TV?

ViewPhoto.TV is a free, browser-based tool that lets users display selected photos from their smartphone on any Smart TV using a QR code. It requires no apps, no accounts, no Wi-Fi pairing, and no screen mirroring. Users choose exactly which photos appear on the TV — their full gallery, notifications, and other private content remain completely hidden.

## How it works (step by step)

1. Open viewphoto.tv in the browser on any Smart TV.
2. A temporary session starts and a unique QR code appears on the TV screen.
3. Scan the QR code with an iPhone or Android phone — no login or installation required.
4. Select photos from the phone's local gallery, Google Photos, or iCloud Photos using the browser's native photo picker.
5. Only the selected photos are uploaded and appear on the TV in a clean, full-screen gallery.
6. The session ends when the TV browser is closed, or automatically after 180 minutes of inactivity.
7. All photos are deleted from Cloudflare storage automatically when the session ends.

## Key features

- Works on any Smart TV with a modern browser: Samsung, LG, Sony, Philips, Google TV, Android TV
- Works on iPhone (Safari, Chrome) and all Android phones
- Photo sources: device storage, Google Photos, iCloud Photos — via the browser's native photo picker
- Multiple users can scan the same QR code and upload photos to the shared screen simultaneously
- Full-screen immersive photo viewer on TV
- No apps on TV or phone
- No accounts or passwords
- No Wi-Fi pairing
- Free to use

## Privacy and security

- Only the photos the user explicitly selects are uploaded — the full photo library is never accessed
- Photos are stored temporarily on Cloudflare R2 / edge cache during the session only
- Default session lifetime: 180 minutes; user can end it at any time
- All photos deleted automatically when the session ends — no permanent storage
- Photos are not analyzed, indexed, or used for AI training
- No personal data collected (no names, emails, phone numbers, identifiers)
- No advertising cookies or tracking SDKs
- All communication encrypted via HTTPS
- QR codes contain only a temporary session ID and expire automatically

## Comparison with screen sharing alternatives

ViewPhoto.TV vs AirPlay: AirPlay mirrors the entire phone screen including notifications and the full photo gallery. ViewPhoto.TV shows only selected photos.

ViewPhoto.TV vs Miracast: Miracast mirrors the entire Android screen. ViewPhoto.TV shows only selected photos and works on iPhone too.

ViewPhoto.TV vs HDMI: HDMI requires a cable and mirrors the entire screen. ViewPhoto.TV is wireless and shows only selected photos.

ViewPhoto.TV vs Chromecast: Chromecast requires the same Wi-Fi network and a compatible app. ViewPhoto.TV works on any Smart TV browser with no setup.

## Use cases

- Showing travel photos or vacation memories on a big screen
- Sharing wedding, birthday, or event photos with guests in real time
- Family gatherings where multiple people contribute photos
- Corporate events and presentations with shared photo feeds
- Hotel rooms or public screens with a Smart TV browser

## Frequently asked questions

**Do I need to install an app?**
No. Everything works in the browser — on both the TV and the phone.

**Does it work on any TV?**
Yes. Any Smart TV with a modern browser: Samsung, LG, Sony, Philips, Google TV, Android TV. Also works on hotel TVs and public screens with a browser.

**Does it work on iPhone?**
Yes. Works with Safari and Chrome on any iPhone.

**Does it work on Android?**
Yes. Works on Samsung, Pixel, Xiaomi, Huawei, Oppo, OnePlus, and all other Android phones.

**Can I use Google Photos or iCloud Photos?**
Yes. Select photos directly from Google Photos or iCloud Photos using the browser's native photo picker — no extra login or app required.

**Can multiple people upload photos?**
Yes. Anyone who scans the QR code can join the session and upload photos to the shared TV screen.

**How long does a session last?**
Sessions expire after 180 minutes of inactivity by default. The host can end the session at any time. Closing the TV browser ends the session immediately.

**Where are my photos stored?**
Photos are stored temporarily on Cloudflare infrastructure (R2 / edge cache) for the duration of the session only. They are deleted automatically when the session ends. No permanent storage.

**Does Cloudflare analyze my photos?**
No. Cloudflare acts as a temporary transport layer only. Photos are not analyzed, indexed, or used for any purpose.

**Do you collect personal data?**
No. No names, emails, phone numbers, advertising identifiers, or any personal information is collected.

**Is the service free?**
Yes. Core functionality is free with no registration.

**Does it support videos?**
Not yet. Video support is planned.

**Can I use it at a wedding or party?**
Yes. This is one of the most popular use cases. Guests can each scan the QR code and add their own photos to the shared screen in real time.

## Technical details

- Infrastructure: Cloudflare R2 for temporary photo storage; Cloudflare edge for delivery
- Session ID: random, temporary, embedded in QR code only
- Transport: HTTPS only
- Photo picker: uses the browser's native file/media picker API — no custom code accesses the photo library
- TV browser compatibility: any modern Smart TV browser (Chromium-based, WebKit-based)
- No service workers, no background sync, no persistent local storage of photos

## Author

ViewPhoto.TV was created by Vadim Sazanovich, Amaryllis Polska.
